warmup: If CP = Rs 250 and SP = Rs 300, what is the profit and profit%? Then: if CP = Rs 500 and profit% = 12%, what is the SP?

practice.title: Cycle Transaction Profit Calculator
practice.description: A dealer buys a bicycle for Rs 2,500 and sells it at a 12% profit. Write a function that computes the SP and profit amount. Then simulate: if he had sold it at a 5% loss instead, what would the SP and loss amount be?
practice.signature: compute_profit_loss(cp, profit_or_loss_pct) and compute_sp_from_cp(cp, pct, is_profit=True)

practice.title: Dishonest Shopkeeper Weight Cheat
practice.description: A vendor claims to sell rice at cost price but uses a weighing scale that shows 1 kg while actually showing 920 gms. Write a function to compute the vendor's actual profit%. Then simulate: if the real CP of rice is Rs 60/kg, what revenue and profit does he make on each '1 kg' sold?

practice.title: Successive Chain of Sales
practice.description: Goods pass through a chain: Manufacturer → Agent (20% profit) → Wholesaler (15% profit) → Retailer (10% loss). If the manufacturer's CP was Rs 5,000, write a function to compute the final SP. Then: what is the overall % change from the original CP?

practice.title: Discount Stacking Comparison
practice.description: An item has MP = Rs 2,000. Option A: two successive discounts of 15% each. Option B: a single discount of 28%. Write a function to compute the SP under both options and determine which gives a better deal to the buyer. Return the difference in SP.

practice.title: Recovery After Loss
practice.description: A shopkeeper sells two items for Rs 1,200 each. On one item he gains 20% and on the other he loses 20%. Write a function to compute the CP of each item, and then determine the net profit or loss on the whole transaction. Was it a loss despite equal SPs?
